title: dasiphora fruticosa subsp. floribunda
intro: Dasiphora fruticosa subsp. floribunda, also known as the shrubby cinquefoil, is a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Dasiphora fruticosa subsp. floribunda include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant effects. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, colds, and other upper respiratory tract infections.
